change_reason: Create GOV-DOCUMENT-AUTHOR-PROVENANCE-001 per bridge gtkb-document-author-provenance-contract GO and DELIB-20260666
explicit_change_request: AUQ DELIB-20260666: Owner authorized document author provenance contract adoption and project-scoped implementation; all five governed surfaces; forward-only posture; new GOV home.
full_content:
## Purpose

GroundTruth-KB document artifacts must carry enough author provenance for
cross-harness auditability when new governed Markdown artifacts are created.

## Contract

New Markdown files created in the governed surfaces must include these six
non-placeholder fields:

- `author_identity`
- `author_harness_id`
- `author_session_context_id`
- `author_model`
- `author_model_version`
- `author_model_configuration`

The governed surfaces are:

- `bridge/**/*.md`
- `.claude/rules/**/*.md`
- `independent-progress-assessments/**/*.md`
- `memory/**/*.md`
- `docs/**/*.md`

The contract is forward-only. Existing files at implementation time are
grandfathered and are not required to be backfilled by this specification.

## Exclusions And Waivers

Historical archives, generated worktree mirrors, and explicitly superseded
artifact folders may be excluded by
`config/governance/document-author-provenance.toml`.

A new governed document may carry a
`document_author_provenance_waiver: <DELIB-ID> - <reason>` line only when a
durable owner decision or governance artifact explicitly authorizes the
exception.

## Enforcement

The enforcement implementation is deterministic:

- `scripts/document_author_metadata.py` defines the required field set,
  placeholder detection, governed-surface matching, and hook decision logic.
- `scripts/check_document_author_metadata.py` audits governed documents and can
  restrict checks to newly-added git paths.
- `.claude/hooks/document_author_provenance_gate.py` enforces the contract for
  Claude Write operations.
- `.codex/gtkb-hooks/document_author_provenance_gate.py` is the intended Codex
  parity wrapper for apply_patch and shell-mediated document creation where the
  Codex hook surface is writable.

## Verification

Verification must include focused helper/checker/hook tests, ruff lint and
format checks for the implementation files, hook-registration evidence, and
readback evidence that this governance specification exists in MemBase.
